item.title: Brian Wilson
item.description: Genres: Baroque pop, sunshine pop, pop rock, psychedelic pop, progressive pop Scene: Hawthorne / Hollywood, California Era: 1962–2022 (primary period: 1963–1967) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The opening chord of “God Only Knows” (1966) — an A major with a suspended fourth dropping to a third over a b...

item.title: Carole King
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, singer-songwriter, Girl group (as songwriter), folk pop Scenes: Brill Building (1958–1967), Laurel Canyon (1968–1975) Era: 1958–present (primary period: 1960–1971) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The opening chord of “I Feel the Earth Move” is a left-hand piano pattern that could be R&...

item.title: Chuck Berry
item.description: Genres: Rock & roll, rhythm & blues Scene: Chicago (Chess Records) Era: 1955–1964 (primary period) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The intro to “Johnny B.

item.title: Laura Nyro
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, singer-songwriter, progressive soul Scene: Brill Building (adjacent), New York City Era: 1966–1997 (primary period: 1967–1971) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The piano on “Timer” (1968) shifts from a ballad’s tenderness to a percussive, almost violent intensity in the space of a breat...

item.title: Burt Bacharach
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, traditional pop, film score Scene: Brill Building Era: 1957–2023 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Burt Bacharach is the most harmonically sophisticated songwriter in the history of American pop — the figure who brought Ravel, Debussy, and Brazilian bossa nova into the three-minute singl...

item.title: Holland-Dozier-Holland
item.description: Era: 1962–1967 (Motown); 1968–present (independent) Genres: Soul, Pop soul, Girl group Key collaborators: The Supremes, The Four Tops, The Temptations, Martha and the Vandellas RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The most prolific and commercially successful songwriting-production team in the history of Mot...

item.title: Lennon-McCartney
item.description: Era: 1957–1970 Genres: Pop rock, Merseybeat, psychedelic pop, art rock Key collaborators: George Martin (producer), The Beatles (performing vehicle) RYM (Lennon) · RYM (McCartney) · Apple Music (Lennon) · Apple Music (McCartney) · Spotify (Lennon) · Spotify (McCartney) John Lennon and Paul McCartney...

item.title: The Kinks
item.description: Genres: Pop rock, Mod, baroque pop, art rock Scene: Mod, North London Era: 1964–1996 (primary period: 1964–1970) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Dave Davies slashed his amplifier speaker with a razor blade and the sound that came out — a ragged, distorted roar underpinning the riff on “You Really Got Me...

item.title: The Ronettes
item.description: Genres: Girl group Scene: Brill Building Era: 1963–1966 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The Ronettes are the Girl group at its most potent — the act that demonstrated what happened when Brill Building songwriting craft and Phil Spector’s Wall of Sound production met a voice strong enough to justify both...

item.title: The Temptations
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul, psychedelic soul Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1960–present (classic era 1964–1971) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The Motown act that best illustrates the label’s capacity for reinvention.
